claims.statement: Patch benchmark schema v12 and matrix schema v6 add canonical inline-string count and bytes while retaining aggregate inline-string, parser, complete-cell, pass-through batching, output, relationship, temporary-IO, CRC and package-writer telemetry.

On this machine and byte-identical one-million-cell mixed-inline sources, the recorded back-to-back schema-v11 to schema-v12 A/B reduced total editor, transform and source scan/action medians by 14.36%, 24.71% and 22.62%.

All 333327 completed simple inline-string payloads in the optimized mixed-inline representative used the canonical literal path, six window-boundary candidates retained fallback, median process peak working set changed by approximately +0.004 MB, and numeric plus formula-hyperlink guards reported zero canonical traffic.

Production test coverage passed all 132 cases across split runs, stored CTest passed 132/132 in one run, and the no-images runtime smoke passed. Representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2 and the formula guard preserved https://example.com/fastxlsx-benchmark. Office was not run; numeric and formula guard timings are excluded, and no cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, full-feature or OpenXLSX-superiority claim is made.
